title: Control characters should be avoided in literals
description: Control characters should not be used in literalsAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEpitfallWhy is this an issue?More InfoControl characters (e.g., tabs or carriage returns) are not visible to maintainers, so they should be escaped.Noncompliant code exampleconst char* foo = "A B"; // Noncompliant: contains a tabulationCompliant solutionconst char* foo = "A\tB"; // Compliant: use escaped valueExceptionsThis rule does not raise issues in raw string literals since they have no escape character mechanism.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025S
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: const char* foo = "A	B";  // Noncompliant: contains a tabulation
compliant: const char* foo = "A\tB";  // Compliant: use escaped value

checks.message: Control characters should be avoided in literals. Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
